title: Author a custom Starlark rule
description: A procedure to write a rule that declares inputs and outputs explicitly so its actions are hermetic and cacheable.
body: Use when built-in rules cannot express a needed build step. Do not shell out to undeclared tools inside the rule. Steps: define the rule with attrs for inputs; in the implementation declare outputs and register actions with explicit inputs and a pinned tool; return providers so consumers can depend on it. Validation signals include the rule's action being cacheable and consumers building through its providers. Risk boundary: a rule that reads undeclared files is non-hermetic and poisons the cache, so declare every input and tool the action uses.
